scenarios.title: enters text into a browser prompt dialog and verifies the message @positive
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to the dialog-boxes demo page, click the prompt button, verify the browser prompt dialog shows 'Please enter your name', type 'John Doe' and accept. (From source: testPrompt — two-step alert acquisition.)
expectedAssertions: dialog message equals 'Please enter your name' before acceptance
dialog accepted with input text 'John Doe'
post-dialog DOM change is visible (Q3 open — reviewer must confirm the observable outcome)

scenarios.title: enters text into a browser prompt dialog (wait-and-assign variant) @positive
scenarios.userAction: Same user-facing scenario as 1.1 — distinct only in Selenium API usage (wait.until returns Alert directly). Behavioral duplicate; see Q1 for consolidation recommendation. (From source: testPrompt2.)
expectedAssertions: dialog message equals 'Please enter your name' before acceptance
dialog accepted with input text 'John Doe'
post-dialog DOM change is visible (Q3 open — reviewer must confirm the observable outcome)

locatorTable.notes: By.id maps mechanically to CSS #id selector per KB §6 pin rule 1. ID is hand-written (not framework-generated). WHY-comment required in Stage 2 output per migration-rules §5 (CSS locator as last resort) unless reviewer confirms role-based upgrade via Q2.

Button role inferred from test context (click triggers a native dialog). Accessible name /prompt/i inferred from element id value — not DOM-confirmed. Reviewer must inspect the page accessibility tree before Stage 2 adopts this. See hallucination-defense pin 1.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q2 unresolved: prompt button accessible name not DOM-confirmed — accessible name /prompt/i inferred from element id value only.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Inspect https://bonigarcia.dev/selenium-webdriver-java/dialog-boxes.html accessibility tree for the button with id='my-prompt'. If name does not match /prompt/i, keep page.locator('#my-prompt') and add a WHY-comment, or ask the page owner to add data-testid='prompt-button'.
